content:
# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: pulp-platform/axi
项目名: axi
讲义目录: axi-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/
当前 HEAD: e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。


## 完整大纲

```json
{
  "project": "axi",
  "rationale": "本项目是 pulp-platform 维护的大型 SystemVerilog AXI4/AXI4-Lite IP 库（88 个源文件，Bender 依赖分为 Level 0–6），因此学习路线直接对齐其天然的依赖层级：先打基础再向上组合。整体分为三层共 16 个单元约 50 篇讲义。入门层（U1–U3）从项目定位、构建系统（Bender/Makefile/scripts）、AXI4 协议回顾出发，再讲三大基础设施——axi_pkg 类型常量、axi_intf 接口与 typedef/assign/port 宏，最后是 axi_test 验证工具与测试台写法，让读者能「看懂、能跑」。进阶层（U4–U14）按模块类别逐族展开：总线连接原语（join/cut/delayer）、多路复用核心（demux/mux）、全连接交叉开关 xbar、流控缓冲（fifo/isolate/throttle）、时钟域跨越（cdc）、突发与序列化、ID 宽度管理、数据宽度转换、AXI-Lite 子系统、协议转换桥、存储端点适配器。每一族都从最底层模块讲到其组合体，遵循「先零件后整机」。专家层（U15–U16）聚焦原子操作 ATOPs、交叉点 xp/interleaved_xbar、异构网络设计取舍、定时与流水线策略、验证方法学和 CI/EDA 兼容等架构级议题。讲义之间通过 depends_on 显式约束先后，避免读者跳读缺失前置知识。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "项目入门与整体认知",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "项目定位与设计哲学",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "介绍 pulp-platform/axi 是什么：一套用于构建片上通信网络的 SystemVerilog AXI4/AXI4-Lite IP 库，以及它的四大设计目标（拓扑无关、模块化组合、适配异构网络、完全协议合规）。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解本库解决什么问题、与手写 AXI 互联的区别",
            "掌握拓扑无关、组合优于配置的 Unix 哲学在硬件中的应用",
            "认识 AXI4+ATOPs 的含义及库内模块的整体分类"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/axi_xbar.sv"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "README",
            "axi_xbar"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 README 的模块列表，按「互连」「转换」「端点」「验证」四类给其中 10 个模块归类，并写一段话说明你会用哪些模块拼出一个两主一从的小型互联。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l2",
          "title": "仓库结构与构建系统",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u1-l2-repo-and-build.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解仓库目录组织（src/include/test/doc/scripts）以及 Bender 包管理器如何用依赖层级（Level 0–6）定义编译顺序，并介绍 src_files.yml、axi.core、ips_list.yml 等清单文件的作用。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "看懂 Bender.yml 中 sources 的层级化组织及其含义",
            "理解 include/ 下三类 .svh 宏文件如何被导出",
            "掌握 src/、test/、scripts/ 各自承担的职责"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Bender.yml",
            "src_files.yml",
            "axi.core",
            "ips_list.yml"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "Bender.yml",
            "src_files.yml"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用 Bender.yml 的层级说明 axi_xbar（Level 5）依赖哪些更低层级的文件，并解释为什么 axi_pkg 必须是 Level 0。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l3",
          "title": "AXI4 协议快速回顾",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u1-l3-axi-protocol-primer.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "用本库源码视角回顾 AXI4：五个通道（AW/W/B/AR/R）、valid/ready 握手、突发类型与响应码，以及「in flight / pending」等术语，为后续阅读模块打协议基础。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "说出五个 AXI 通道及其数据流方向",
            "理解 valid/ready 握手与 in flight、pending 的精确定义",
            "区分 BURST_FIXED/INCR/WRAP 与 RESP_OKAY/EXOKAY/SLVERR/DECERR"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/axi_pkg.sv",
            "doc/README.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "axi_pkg",
            "doc/README"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在 axi_pkg.sv 中找到 BURST_* 与 RESP_* 的 localparam 定义，画出一次 4 拍 INCR 写事务在五个通道上的时序草图。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l4",
          "title": "如何编译、仿真与综合",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u1-l4-compile-sim-synth.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解如何用 Makefile 与 scripts/ 下的脚本完成编译（compile_vsim.sh）、仿真（run_vsim.sh）、Verilator lint（run_verilator.sh）与综合（synth.sh），以及 test/ 目录的 testbench 命名约定。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "用 make compile.log / sim-<tb>.log / elab.log 跑通流程",
            "理解 Bender target（rtl/test/simulation/synthesis/synth_test）的作用",
            "知道 test/ 下 tb_*.sv 与对应 DUT 的对应关系"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Makefile",
            "scripts/compile_vsim.sh",
            "scripts/run_vsim.sh",
            "scripts/synth.sh",
            "scripts/run_verilator.sh"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "Makefile",
            "scripts"
          ],
          "practice_task": "执行 make help 查看可用目标，然后选择一个 testbench（如 tb_axi_lite_regs）跑一次仿真并解释日志中「Errors: 0」的含义。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u2",
      "title": "基础设施 — 类型、接口与宏",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 2,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u2-l1",
          "title": "axi_pkg：类型与常量",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u2-l1-axi-pkg-types.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "精读 axi_pkg.sv 的参数化宽度常量（BurstWidth、RespWidth 等）、typedef（burst_t、resp_t、atop_t 等）以及 BURST/RESP/CACHE 等 localparam，这是全库共享的类型根基。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 axi_pkg 中所有公共 typedef 与 localparam 的来源",
            "理解为什么把这些类型集中在一个 package 里",
            "能引用 axi_pkg:: 下的类型来声明自己的信号"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/axi_pkg.sv"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "axi_pkg"
          ],
          "practice_task": "列出 axi_pkg 中每个 typedef 对应的位宽常量，并写一小段 SystemVerilog 用 axi_pkg::resp_t 和 RESP_DECERR 声明并赋值一个信号。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l2",
          "title": "axi_pkg：辅助函数与配置结构体",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u2-l2-axi-pkg-funcs-cfg.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 axi_pkg 中的辅助函数（如 num_bytes、地址对齐计算）以及 xbar_cfg_t、xbar_rule_64_t/32_t、xbar_latency_e 等配置结构体，它们是参数化模块（尤其 xbar）的配置入口。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "使用 axi_pkg 的函数完成字节/拍数计算",
            "看懂 xbar_cfg_t 各字段含义并能构造一个配置",
            "理解 xbar_latency_e 枚举（CUT_ALL_AX、NO_LATENCY 等）的用途"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/axi_pkg.sv",
            "doc/axi_xbar.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "axi_pkg",
            "doc/axi_xbar"
          ],
          "practice_task": "对照 doc/axi_xbar.md 的字段表，为「2 个 slave 端口、3 个 master 端口」写出一个 xbar_cfg_t 实例，并选择合适的 LatencyMode。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l3",
          "title": "axi_intf：SystemVerilog 接口",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u2-l3-axi-intf.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "精读 axi_intf.sv 中定义的 AXI_BUS、AXI_BUS_DV（带时钟的验证用接口）、AXI_LITE 等接口及其 Master/Slave/Monitor modport，理解接口如何封装五通道信号。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 AXI_BUS 接口的 modport 如何区分主从监听",
            "掌握 AXI_BUS_DV 引入 clk_i 的用途",
            "能在一个模块端口上正确使用 AXI_BUS.Slave"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/axi_intf.sv"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "axi_intf"
          ],
          "practice_task": "基于 AXI_BUS_DV 写一个最小的 monitor 模块，用 Monitor modport 端口把 AW 通道的 id/addr 打印出来。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l4",
          "title": "typedef / assign / port 宏体系",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u2-l4-typedef-assign-port-macros.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 include/axi/ 下三大宏文件：typedef.svh（声明通道/请求/响应 struct）、assign.svh（在接口与 struct 间互连）、port.svh（生成扁平 AXI 端口），它们是减少重复样板代码的关键。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "用 AXI_TYPEDEF_* 宏声明一组通道与 req/resp struct",
            "用 AXI_ASSIGN_* 宏在 AXI_BUS 接口与 struct 之间搬数据",
            "理解 port.svh 宏在顶层扁平化端口时的作用"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "include/axi/typedef.svh",
            "include/axi/assign.svh",
            "include/axi/port.svh",
            "src/axi_xbar.sv"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "typedef.svh",
            "assign.svh",
            "port.svh"
          ],
          "practice_task": "仿照 axi_xbar_intf 中的写法，用 AXI_TYPEDEF_* 与 AXI_ASSIGN_* 宏为一个自定义模块声明 req/resp 类型并把它们连到 AXI_BUS 接口。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l2",
            "u2-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u3",
      "title": "仿真与验证基础设施",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 3,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u3-l1",
          "title": "axi_test：底层驱动",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u3-l1-axi-test-drivers.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 axi_test.sv 中的 axi_lite_driver 与 axi_driver 类：如何用 send_aw/send_w recv_b 等任务在通道上逐拍驱动，以及 TA/TT 时序参数的含义。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "用 driver 类发送单拍握手并理解 application/test time",
            "掌握 reset_master/reset_slave 的初始化套路",
            "理解 driver 与 AXI_BUS_DV 虚接口的绑定方式"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/axi_test.sv"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "axi_test"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用 axi_lite_driver 写一段过程：发起一次 32 位写、再发起一次读并检查返回数据，标注 TA/TT 的作用。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l3",
            "u2-l4"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l2",
          "title": "随机主从、scoreboard 与 sim_mem",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u3-l2-rand-master-slave-scoreboard.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 axi_test 中的 axi_rand_master/axi_rand_slave/axi_lite_rand_*、axi_scoreboard 以及 axi_sim_mem，它们是构建随机激励与自检测试台的核心组件。",
          "learning_goals": [

## 本讲义规格

- id: u1-l1
- 文件名: u1-l1-project-overview.md
- 写入路径: axi-tutorial/u1-l1-project-overview.md
- 标题: 项目定位与设计哲学
- 学习阶段: beginner
- 主题: 介绍 pulp-platform/axi 是什么：一套用于构建片上通信网络的 SystemVerilog AXI4/AXI4-Lite IP 库，以及它的四大设计目标（拓扑无关、模块化组合、适配异构网络、完全协议合规）。
- 学习目标: 理解本库解决什么问题、与手写 AXI 互联的区别；掌握拓扑无关、组合优于配置的 Unix 哲学在硬件中的应用；认识 AXI4+ATOPs 的含义及库内模块的整体分类
- 应覆盖的最小模块: README，axi_xbar
- 关键源码: README.md，src/axi_xbar.sv
- 代码实践任务: 阅读 README 的模块列表，按「互连」「转换」「端点」「验证」四类给其中 10 个模块归类，并写一段话说明你会用哪些模块拼出一个两主一从的小型互联。
- 依赖讲义: 无

---

## 任务

请按照 worker prompt 生成这一篇讲义。

要求：

1. 只生成 `axi-tutorial/u1-l1-project-overview.md` 这一个文件。
2. 必须结合真实源码。
3. 必须包含代码实践。
4. 必须使用永久链接引用源码。
5. new/rebuild 模式下从零写入。
6. update 模式下先读取旧文件，再结合 diff 更新。
7. 不要修改源码。
8. 不要写其他文件。

完成后，用一句话总结本讲义覆盖的最小模块。

message.content:
# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: pulp-platform/axi
项目名: axi
讲义目录: axi-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/
当前 HEAD: e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。


## 完整大纲

```json
{
  "project": "axi",
  "rationale": "本项目是 pulp-platform 维护的大型 SystemVerilog AXI4/AXI4-Lite IP 库（88 个源文件，Bender 依赖分为 Level 0–6），因此学习路线直接对齐其天然的依赖层级：先打基础再向上组合。整体分为三层共 16 个单元约 50 篇讲义。入门层（U1–U3）从项目定位、构建系统（Bender/Makefile/scripts）、AXI4 协议回顾出发，再讲三大基础设施——axi_pkg 类型常量、axi_intf 接口与 typedef/assign/port 宏，最后是 axi_test 验证工具与测试台写法，让读者能「看懂、能跑」。进阶层（U4–U14）按模块类别逐族展开：总线连接原语（join/cut/delayer）、多路复用核心（demux/mux）、全连接交叉开关 xbar、流控缓冲（fifo/isolate/throttle）、时钟域跨越（cdc）、突发与序列化、ID 宽度管理、数据宽度转换、AXI-Lite 子系统、协议转换桥、存储端点适配器。每一族都从最底层模块讲到其组合体，遵循「先零件后整机」。专家层（U15–U16）聚焦原子操作 ATOPs、交叉点 xp/interleaved_xbar、异构网络设计取舍、定时与流水线策略、验证方法学和 CI/EDA 兼容等架构级议题。讲义之间通过 depends_on 显式约束先后，避免读者跳读缺失前置知识。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "项目入门与整体认知",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "项目定位与设计哲学",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "介绍 pulp-platform/axi 是什么：一套用于构建片上通信网络的 SystemVerilog AXI4/AXI4-Lite IP 库，以及它的四大设计目标（拓扑无关、模块化组合、适配异构网络、完全协议合规）。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解本库解决什么问题、与手写 AXI 互联的区别",
            "掌握拓扑无关、组合优于配置的 Unix 哲学在硬件中的应用",
            "认识 AXI4+ATOPs 的含义及库内模块的整体分类"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/axi_xbar.sv"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [

          "practice_task": "阅读 README 的模块列表，按「互连」「转换」「端点」「验证」四类给其中 10 个模块归类，并写一段话说明你会用哪些模块拼出一个两主一从的小型互联。",

content.content:
1	# AXI SystemVerilog Modules for High-Performance On-Chip Communication
2	[![CI status](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/actions/workflows/gitlab-ci.yml/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/actions/workflows/gitlab-ci.yml?query=branch%3Amaster)
3	[![GitHub tag (latest SemVer)](https://img.shields.io/github/v/tag/pulp-platform/axi?color=blue&label=current&sort=semver)](CHANGELOG.md)
4	[![SHL-0.51 license](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-SHL--0.51-green)](LICENSE)
5	
6	This repository provides modules to build on-chip communication networks adhering to the [AXI4 or AXI4-Lite standards][AMBA 5 Spec].  For high-performance communication, we implement AXI4[+ATOPs from AXI5](#atomic-operations).  For lightweight communication, we implement AXI4-Lite.  We aim to provide a complete end-to-end communication platform, including endpoints such as DMA engines and on-chip memory controllers.
7	
8	Our **design goals** are:
9	- **Topology Independence**: We provide elementary building blocks such as protocol [multiplexers](src/axi_mux.sv) and [demultiplexers](src/axi_demux.sv) that allow users to implement any network topology.  We also provide commonly used interconnecting components such as a [crossbar](src/axi_xbar.sv).
10	- **Modularity**: We favor design by composition over design by configuration where possible.  We strive to apply the *Unix philosophy* to hardware: make each module do one thing well.  This means you will more often instantiate our modules back-to-back than change a parameter value to build more specialized networks.
11	- **Fit for Heterogeneous Networks**: Our modules are parametrizable in terms of data width and transaction concurrency.  This allows to create optimized networks for a wide range of performance (e.g., bandwidth, concurrency, timing), power, and area requirements.  We provide modules such as [data width converters](src/axi_dw_converter.sv) and [ID width converters](src/axi_iw_converter.sv) that allow to join subnetworks with different properties, creating heterogeneous on-chip networks.
12	- **Full AXI Standard Compliance**.
13	- **Compatibility** with a [wide range of (recent versions of) EDA tools](#which-eda-tools-are-supported) and implementation in standardized synthesizable SystemVerilog.
14	
15	The **design and microarchitecture** of the modules in this repository is described in [**this paper**](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9522037) ([preprint](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2009.05334)).  If you use our work in your research, please cite it.
16	
17	
18	## List of Modules
19	
20	In addition to the documents linked in the following table, we are setting up [documentation auto-generated from inline docstrings](https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master).  (Replace `master` in that URL with a tag to get the documentation for a specific version.)
21	
22	| Name                                                        | Description                                                                                          | Doc                              |
23	|-------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------|
24	| [`axi_atop_filter`](src/axi_atop_filter.sv)                 | Filters atomic operations (ATOPs), i.e., write transactions that have a non-zero `aw_atop` value.    |                                  |
25	| [`axi_burst_splitter`](src/axi_burst_splitter.sv)           | Split AXI4 burst transfers into single-beat transactions.                                            |                                  |
26	| [`axi_burst_splitter_gran`](src/axi_burst_splitter_gran.sv) | Split AXI4 burst transfers into transactions of runtime-configurable granularity.                    |                                  |
27	| [`axi_burst_unwrap`](src/axi_burst_unwrap.sv)               | Convert AXI4 wrapping burst transfers into up to two incremental bursts.                             |                                  |
28	| [`axi_cdc`](src/axi_cdc.sv)                                 | AXI clock domain crossing based on a Gray FIFO implementation.                                       |                                  |
29	| [`axi_cut`](src/axi_cut.sv)                                 | Breaks all combinatorial paths between its input and output.                                         |                                  |
30	| [`axi_delayer`](src/axi_delayer.sv)                         | Synthesizable module which can (randomly) delays AXI channels.                                       |                                  |
31	| [`axi_demux_simple`](src/axi_demux_simple.sv)               | Demux without spill registers.                                                                       | [Doc](doc/axi_demux.md)          |
32	| [`axi_demux`](src/axi_demux.sv)                             | Demultiplexes an AXI bus from one slave port to multiple master ports.                               | [Doc](doc/axi_demux.md)          |
33	| [`axi_demux_id_counters`](src/axi_demux_id_counters.sv)     | ID counters used by `axi_demux` to track outstanding transactions.                                   |                                  |
34	| [`axi_dw_converter`](src/axi_dw_converter.sv)               | A data width converter between AXI interfaces of any data width.                                     |                                  |
35	| [`axi_dw_downsizer`](src/axi_dw_downsizer.sv)               | A data width converter between a wide AXI master and a narrower AXI slave.                           |                                  |
36	| [`axi_dw_upsizer`](src/axi_dw_upsizer.sv)                   | A data width converter between a narrow AXI master and a wider AXI slave.                            |                                  |
37	| [`axi_err_slv`](src/axi_err_slv.sv)                         | Always responds with an AXI decode/slave error for transactions which are sent to it.                |                                  |
38	| [`axi_fifo`](src/axi_fifo.sv)                               | A Fifo for each AXI4 channel to buffer requests.                                                     |                                  |
39	| [`axi_from_mem`](src/axi_from_mem.sv)                       | This module acts like an SRAM and makes AXI4 requests downstream.                                    |                                  |
40	| [`axi_id_prepend`](src/axi_id_prepend.sv)                   | This module prepends/strips the MSB from the AXI IDs.                                                |                                  |
41	| [`axi_id_remap`](src/axi_id_remap.sv)                       | Remap AXI IDs from wide IDs at the slave port to narrower IDs at the master port.                    | [Doc][doc.axi_id_remap]          |
42	| [`axi_id_serialize`](src/axi_id_serialize.sv)               | Reduce AXI IDs by serializing transactions when necessary.                                           | [Doc][doc.axi_id_serialize]      |
43	| [`axi_interleaved_xbar`](src/axi_interleaved_xbar.sv)       | Interleaved version of the crossbar. This module is experimental; use at your own risk.              |                                  |
44	| [`axi_intf`](src/axi_intf.sv)                               | This file defines the interfaces we support.                                                         |                                  |
45	| [`axi_inval_filter`](src/axi_inval_filter.sv)               | Listens to AXI4 AW channel and issues single cacheline invalidations.                                |                                  |
46	| [`axi_isolate`](src/axi_isolate.sv)                         | A module that can isolate downstream slaves from receiving new AXI4 transactions.                    |                                  |
47	| [`axi_iw_converter`](src/axi_iw_converter.sv)               | Convert between any two AXI ID widths.                                                               | [Doc][doc.axi_iw_converter]      |

49	| [`axi_lfsr`](src/axi_lfsr.sv)                               | AXI4-attached LFSR; read returns pseudo-random data, writes are compressed into a checksum.          |                                  |
50	| [`axi_lite_demux`](src/axi_lite_demux.sv)                   | Demultiplexes an AXI4-Lite bus from one slave port to multiple master ports.                         | [Doc](doc/axi_lite_demux.md)     |
51	| [`axi_lite_dw_converter`](src/axi_lite_dw_converter.sv)     | A data width converter between two AXI-Lite busses                                                   | [Doc][doc.axi_lite_dw_converter] |
52	| [`axi_lite_from_mem`](src/axi_lite_from_mem.sv)             | This module acts like an SRAM and makes AXI4-Lite requests downstream.                               |                                  |
53	| [`axi_lite_join`](src/axi_lite_join.sv)                     | A connector that joins two AXI-Lite interfaces.                                                      |                                  |
54	| [`axi_lite_lfsr`](src/axi_lite_lfsr.sv)                     | AXI4-Lite-attached LFSR; read returns pseudo-random data, writes are compressed into a checksum.     |                                  |
55	| [`axi_lite_mailbox`](src/axi_lite_mailbox.sv)               | A AXI4-Lite Mailbox with two slave ports and usage triggered irq.                                    | [Doc](doc/axi_lite_mailbox.md)   |
56	| [`axi_lite_mux`](src/axi_lite_mux.sv)                       | Multiplexes AXI4-Lite slave ports down to one master port.                                           | [Doc](doc/axi_lite_mux.md)       |
57	| [`axi_lite_regs`](src/axi_lite_regs.sv)                     | AXI4-Lite registers with optional read-only and protection features.                                 | [Doc][doc.axi_lite_regs]         |
58	| [`axi_lite_to_apb`](src/axi_lite_to_apb.sv)                 | AXI4-Lite to APB4 protocol converter.                                                                |                                  |
59	| [`axi_lite_to_axi`](src/axi_lite_to_axi.sv)                 | AXI4-Lite to AXI4 protocol converter.                                                                |                                  |
60	| [`axi_lite_xbar`](src/axi_lite_xbar.sv)                     | Fully-connected AXI4-Lite crossbar with an arbitrary number of slave and master ports.               | [Doc](doc/axi_lite_xbar.md)      |
61	| [`axi_modify_address`](src/axi_modify_address.sv)           | A connector that allows addresses of AXI requests to be changed.                                     |                                  |
62	| [`axi_multicut`](src/axi_multicut.sv)                       | AXI register which can be used to relax timing pressure on long AXI buses.                           |                                  |
63	| [`axi_mux`](src/axi_mux.sv)                                 | Multiplexes the AXI4 slave ports down to one master port.                                            | [Doc](doc/axi_mux.md)            |
64	| [`axi_pkg`](src/axi_pkg.sv)                                 | Contains AXI definitions, common structs, and useful helper functions.                               |                                  |
65	| [`axi_rw_join`](src/axi_rw_join.sv)                         | Joins a read and a write slave into one single read / write master.                                  |                                  |

73	| [`axi_xbar_unmuxed`](src/axi_xbar_unmuxed.sv)               | Demux side of fully-connected AXI4+ATOP crossbar with an arbitrary number of slave and master ports. | [Doc](doc/axi_xbar.md)           |

76	
77	## Synthesizable Verification Modules
78	
79	The following modules are meant to be used for verification purposes only but are synthesizable to be used in FPGA environments.
80	
81	| Name                                                 | Description                                                                                             |
82	|------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
83	| [`axi_bus_compare`](src/axi_bus_compare.sv)          | Compares two buses of the same type (and in the same clock domain), returns events on mismatch.         |
84	| [`axi_fifo_delay_dyn`](src/axi_fifo_delay_dyn.sv)    | Delay and buffer each channel of an AXI bus individually.                                               |
85	| [`axi_slave_compare`](src/axi_slave_compare.sv)      | Compares two slave devices of the same type (and in the same clock domain), returns events on mismatch. |
86	
87	
88	### Simulation-Only Modules
89	
90	In addition to the modules above, which are available in synthesis and simulation, the following modules are available only in simulation.  Those modules are widely used in our testbenches, but they are also suitable to build testbenches for AXI modules and systems outside this repository.
91	
92	| Name                                                 | Description                                                                                            |
93	|------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
94	| [`axi_chan_compare`](src/axi_chan_compare.sv)        | Non-synthesizable module comparing two AXI channels of the same type                                   |
95	| [`axi_chan_logger`](src/axi_test.sv)                 | Logs the transactions of an AXI4(+ATOPs) port to files.                                                |
96	| [`axi_driver`](src/axi_test.sv)                      | Low-level driver for AXI4(+ATOPs) that can send and receive individual beats on any channel.           |
97	| [`axi_dumper`](src/axi_dumper.sv)                    | Dumps log to file to be interpreted by `axi_dumper_interpret` script for debugging purposes.           |
98	| [`axi_file_master`](src/axi_test.sv)                 | AXI4 master for file-based testbenches                                                                 |
99	| [`axi_lite_driver`](src/axi_test.sv)                 | Low-level driver for AXI4-Lite that can send and receive individual beats on any channel.              |
100	| [`axi_lite_rand_master`](src/axi_test.sv)            | AXI4-Lite master component that issues random transactions within user-defined constraints.            |
101	| [`axi_lite_rand_slave`](src/axi_test.sv)             | AXI4-Lite slave component that responds to transactions with constrainable random delays and data.     |
102	| [`axi_rand_master`](src/axi_test.sv)                 | AXI4(+ATOPs) master component that issues random transactions within user-defined constraints.         |
103	| [`axi_rand_slave`](src/axi_test.sv)                  | AXI4(+ATOPs) slave component that responds to transactions with constrainable random delays and data.  |
104	| [`axi_scoreboard`](src/axi_test.sv)                  | Scoreboard that models a memory that only gets changed by the monitored AXI4(+ATOPs) port.             |
105	| [`axi_sim_mem`](src/axi_sim_mem.sv)                  | Infinite memory with AXI4 slave port.                                                                  |
106	
107	
108	
109	## Atomic Operations
110	
111	AXI4+ATOPs means the full AXI4 specification plus atomic operations (ATOPs) as defined in Section E1.1 of the [AMBA 5 specification][AMBA 5 Spec]. This has the following implications for modules that do not implement ATOPs and systems that include such modules:
112	
113	- Masters that do not issue ATOPs must set `aw_atop` to `'0`.
114	- Slaves that do not support ATOPs must specify this in their interface documentation and can ignore the `aw_atop` signal.
115	- System designers are responsible for ensuring that
116	  1. slaves that do not support ATOPs are behind an [`axi_atop_filter`](src/axi_atop_filter.sv) if any master could issue an ATOP to such slaves and
117	  2. the `aw_atop` signal is well-defined at the input of any (non-AXI4-Lite) module in this repository.
118	
119	Masters and slaves that do support ATOPs must adhere to Section E1.1 of the [AMBA 5 specification][AMBA 5 Spec].  In particular:
120	- ATOPs that have the `aw_atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP]` bit set generate a write response (B channel) beat and at least one read response (R channel) beat.  All modules for which the `aw_atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP]` bit could be set at their master port must be able to handle both B and R beats (in any order and without requiring a simultaneous handshake) for each such ATOP request.  All modules for which the `aw_atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP]` bit could be set at their slave port must respond with the appropriate number of B and R beats for each such ATOP request.
121	- ATOPs must not use the same AXI ID as any other transaction that is outstanding at the same time.
122	
123	
124	## Which EDA Tools Are Supported?
125	
126	Our code is written in standard SystemVerilog ([IEEE 1800-2012][], to be precise), so the more important question is: Which subset of SystemVerilog does your EDA tool support?
127	
128	We aim to be compatible with a wide range of EDA tools.  For this reason, we strive to use as simple language constructs as possible, especially for our synthesizable modules.  We encourage contributions that further simplify our code to make it compatible with even more EDA tools.  We also welcome contributions that work around problems that specific EDA tools may have with our code, as long as:
129	- the EDA tool is reasonably widely used,
130	- recent versions of the EDA tool are affected,
131	- the workaround does not break functionality in other tools, and
132	- the workaround does not significantly complicate code or add maintenance overhead.
133	
134	In addition, we suggest to report issues with the SystemVerilog language support directly to the EDA vendor. Our code is fully open and
135	can / should be shared with the EDA vendor as a testcase for any language problem encountered.
136	
137	All code in each release and on the default branch is tested on a recent version of at least one industry-standard RTL simulator and synthesizer.  You can examine the [CI settings](./.gitlab-ci.yml) to find out which version of which tool we are running.
138	
139	
140	[AMBA 5 Spec]: https://developer.arm.com/documentation/ihi0022/hc
141	[IEEE 1800-2012]: https://standards.ieee.org/standard/1800-2012.html
142	[doc.axi_id_remap]: https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master/module.axi_id_remap
143	[doc.axi_id_serialize]: https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master/module.axi_id_serialize
144	[doc.axi_iw_converter]: https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master/module.axi_iw_converter
145	[doc.axi_lite_regs]: https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master/module.axi_lite_regs
146

# AXI SystemVerilog Modules for High-Performance On-Chip Communication
[![CI status](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/actions/workflows/gitlab-ci.yml/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/actions/workflows/gitlab-ci.yml?query=branch%3Amaster)
[![GitHub tag (latest SemVer)](https://img.shields.io/github/v/tag/pulp-platform/axi?color=blue&label=current&sort=semver)](CHANGELOG.md)
[![SHL-0.51 license](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-SHL--0.51-green)](LICENSE)

This repository provides modules to build on-chip communication networks adhering to the [AXI4 or AXI4-Lite standards][AMBA 5 Spec].  For high-performance communication, we implement AXI4[+ATOPs from AXI5](#atomic-operations).  For lightweight communication, we implement AXI4-Lite.  We aim to provide a complete end-to-end communication platform, including endpoints such as DMA engines and on-chip memory controllers.

Our **design goals** are:
- **Topology Independence**: We provide elementary building blocks such as protocol [multiplexers](src/axi_mux.sv) and [demultiplexers](src/axi_demux.sv) that allow users to implement any network topology.  We also provide commonly used interconnecting components such as a [crossbar](src/axi_xbar.sv).
- **Modularity**: We favor design by composition over design by configuration where possible.  We strive to apply the *Unix philosophy* to hardware: make each module do one thing well.  This means you will more often instantiate our modules back-to-back than change a parameter value to build more specialized networks.
- **Fit for Heterogeneous Networks**: Our modules are parametrizable in terms of data width and transaction concurrency.  This allows to create optimized networks for a wide range of performance (e.g., bandwidth, concurrency, timing), power, and area requirements.  We provide modules such as [data width converters](src/axi_dw_converter.sv) and [ID width converters](src/axi_iw_converter.sv) that allow to join subnetworks with different properties, creating heterogeneous on-chip networks.
- **Full AXI Standard Compliance**.
- **Compatibility** with a [wide range of (recent versions of) EDA tools](#which-eda-tools-are-supported) and implementation in standardized synthesizable SystemVerilog.

The **design and microarchitecture** of the modules in this repository is described in [**this paper**](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9522037) ([preprint](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2009.05334)).  If you use our work in your research, please cite it.


## List of Modules

In addition to the documents linked in the following table, we are setting up [documentation auto-generated from inline docstrings](https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master).  (Replace `master` in that URL with a tag to get the documentation for a specific version.)

| Name                                                        | Description                                                                                          | Doc                              |
|-------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------|
| [`axi_atop_filter`](src/axi_atop_filter.sv)                 | Filters atomic operations (ATOPs), i.e., write transactions that have a non-zero `aw_atop` value.    |                                  |
| [`axi_burst_splitter`](src/axi_burst_splitter.sv)           | Split AXI4 burst transfers into single-beat transactions.                                            |                                  |
| [`axi_burst_splitter_gran`](src/axi_burst_splitter_gran.sv) | Split AXI4 burst transfers into transactions of runtime-configurable granularity.                    |                                  |
| [`axi_burst_unwrap`](src/axi_burst_unwrap.sv)               | Convert AXI4 wrapping burst transfers into up to two incremental bursts.                             |                                  |
| [`axi_cdc`](src/axi_cdc.sv)                                 | AXI clock domain crossing based on a Gray FIFO implementation.                                       |                                  |
| [`axi_cut`](src/axi_cut.sv)                                 | Breaks all combinatorial paths between its input and output.                                         |                                  |
| [`axi_delayer`](src/axi_delayer.sv)                         | Synthesizable module which can (randomly) delays AXI channels.                                       |                                  |
| [`axi_demux_simple`](src/axi_demux_simple.sv)               | Demux without spill registers.                                                                       | [Doc](doc/axi_demux.md)          |
| [`axi_demux`](src/axi_demux.sv)                             | Demultiplexes an AXI bus from one slave port to multiple master ports.                               | [Doc](doc/axi_demux.md)          |
| [`axi_demux_id_counters`](src/axi_demux_id_counters.sv)     | ID counters used by `axi_demux` to track outstanding transactions.                                   |                                  |
| [`axi_dw_converter`](src/axi_dw_converter.sv)               | A data width converter between AXI interfaces of any data width.                                     |                                  |
| [`axi_dw_downsizer`](src/axi_dw_downsizer.sv)               | A data width converter between a wide AXI master and a narrower AXI slave.                           |                                  |
| [`axi_dw_upsizer`](src/axi_dw_upsizer.sv)                   | A data width converter between a narrow AXI master and a wider AXI slave.                            |                                  |
| [`axi_err_slv`](src/axi_err_slv.sv)                         | Always responds with an AXI decode/slave error for transactions which are sent to it.                |                                  |
| [`axi_fifo`](src/axi_fifo.sv)                               | A Fifo for each AXI4 channel to buffer requests.                                                     |                                  |
| [`axi_from_mem`](src/axi_from_mem.sv)                       | This module acts like an SRAM and makes AXI4 requests downstream.                                    |                                  |
| [`axi_id_prepend`](src/axi_id_prepend.sv)                   | This module prepends/strips the MSB from the AXI IDs.                                                |                                  |
| [`axi_id_remap`](src/axi_id_remap.sv)                       | Remap AXI IDs from wide IDs at the slave port to narrower IDs at the master port.                    | [Doc][doc.axi_id_remap]          |
| [`axi_id_serialize`](src/axi_id_serialize.sv)               | Reduce AXI IDs by serializing transactions when necessary.                                           | [Doc][doc.axi_id_serialize]      |
| [`axi_interleaved_xbar`](src/axi_interleaved_xbar.sv)       | Interleaved version of the crossbar. This module is experimental; use at your own risk.              |                                  |
| [`axi_intf`](src/axi_intf.sv)                               | This file defines the interfaces we support.                                                         |                                  |
| [`axi_inval_filter`](src/axi_inval_filter.sv)               | Listens to AXI4 AW channel and issues single cacheline invalidations.                                |                                  |
| [`axi_isolate`](src/axi_isolate.sv)                         | A module that can isolate downstream slaves from receiving new AXI4 transactions.                    |                                  |
| [`axi_iw_converter`](src/axi_iw_converter.sv)               | Convert between any two AXI ID widths.                                                               | [Doc][doc.axi_iw_converter]      |
| [`axi_join`](src/axi_join.sv)                               | A connector that joins two AXI interfaces.                                                           |                                  |
| [`axi_lfsr`](src/axi_lfsr.sv)                               | AXI4-attached LFSR; read returns pseudo-random data, writes are compressed into a checksum.          |                                  |
| [`axi_lite_demux`](src/axi_lite_demux.sv)                   | Demultiplexes an AXI4-Lite bus from one slave port to multiple master ports.                         | [Doc](doc/axi_lite_demux.md)     |
| [`axi_lite_dw_converter`](src/axi_lite_dw_converter.sv)     | A data width converter between two AXI-Lite busses                                                   | [Doc][doc.axi_lite_dw_converter] |
| [`axi_lite_from_mem`](src/axi_lite_from_mem.sv)             | This module acts like an SRAM and makes AXI4-Lite requests downstream.                               |                                  |
| [`axi_lite_join`](src/axi_lite_join.sv)                     | A connector that joins two AXI-Lite interfaces.                                                      |                                  |
| [`axi_lite_lfsr`](src/axi_lite_lfsr.sv)                     | AXI4-Lite-attached LFSR; read returns pseudo-random data, writes are compressed into a checksum.     |                                  |
| [`axi_lite_mailbox`](src/axi_lite_mailbox.sv)               | A AXI4-Lite Mailbox with two slave ports and usage triggered irq.                                    | [Doc](doc/axi_lite_mailbox.md)   |
| [`axi_lite_mux`](src/axi_lite_mux.sv)                       | Multiplexes AXI4-Lite slave ports down to one master port.                                           | [Doc](doc/axi_lite_mux.md)       |
| [`axi_lite_regs`](src/axi_lite_regs.sv)                     | AXI4-Lite registers with optional read-only and protection features.                                 | [Doc][doc.axi_lite_regs]         |
| [`axi_lite_to_apb`](src/axi_lite_to_apb.sv)                 | AXI4-Lite to APB4 protocol converter.                                                                |                                  |
| [`axi_lite_to_axi`](src/axi_lite_to_axi.sv)                 | AXI4-Lite to AXI4 protocol converter.                                                                |                                  |
| [`axi_lite_xbar`](src/axi_lite_xbar.sv)                     | Fully-connected AXI4-Lite crossbar with an arbitrary number of slave and master ports.               | [Doc](doc/axi_lite_xbar.md)      |
| [`axi_modify_address`](src/axi_modify_address.sv)           | A connector that allows addresses of AXI requests to be changed.                                     |                                  |
| [`axi_multicut`](src/axi_multicut.sv)                       | AXI register which can be used to relax timing pressure on long AXI buses.                           |                                  |
| [`axi_mux`](src/axi_mux.sv)                                 | Multiplexes the AXI4 slave ports down to one master port.                                            | [Doc](doc/axi_mux.md)            |
| [`axi_pkg`](src/axi_pkg.sv)                                 | Contains AXI definitions, common structs, and useful helper functions.                               |                                  |
| [`axi_rw_join`](src/axi_rw_join.sv)                         | Joins a read and a write slave into one single read / write master.                                  |                                  |
| [`axi_rw_split`](src/axi_rw_split.sv)                       | Splits a single read / write slave into one read and one write master.                               |                                  |
| [`axi_serializer`](src/axi_serializer.sv)                   | Serializes transactions with different IDs to the same ID.                                           |                                  |
| [`axi_throttle`](src/axi_throttle.sv)                       | Limits the maximum number of outstanding transfers sent to the downstream logic.                     |                                  |
| [`axi_test`](src/axi_test.sv)                               | A set of testbench utilities for AXI interfaces.                                                     |                                  |
| [`axi_to_axi_lite`](src/axi_to_axi_lite.sv)                 | AXI4 to AXI4-Lite protocol converter.                                                                |                                  |
| [`axi_to_mem`](src/axi_to_mem.sv)                           | AXI4 to memory protocol (req, gnt, rvalid) converter. Additional banked, interleaved, split variant. |                                  |
| [`axi_xbar`](src/axi_xbar.sv)                               | Fully-connected AXI4+ATOP crossbar with an arbitrary number of slave and master ports.               | [Doc](doc/axi_xbar.md)           |
| [`axi_xbar_unmuxed`](src/axi_xbar_unmuxed.sv)               | Demux side of fully-connected AXI4+ATOP crossbar with an arbitrary number of slave and master ports. | [Doc](doc/axi_xbar.md)           |
| [`axi_xp`](src/axi_xp.sv)                                   | AXI Crosspoint (XP) with homomorphous slave and master ports.                                        |                                  |
| [`axi_zero_mem`](src/axi_zero_mem.sv)                       | AXI-attached /dev/zero. All reads will be zero, writes are absorbed.                                 |                                  |

## Synthesizable Verification Modules

The following modules are meant to be used for verification purposes only but are synthesizable to be used in FPGA environments.

| Name                                                 | Description                                                                                             |
|------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [`axi_bus_compare`](src/axi_bus_compare.sv)          | Compares two buses of the same type (and in the same clock domain), returns events on mismatch.         |
| [`axi_fifo_delay_dyn`](src/axi_fifo_delay_dyn.sv)    | Delay and buffer each channel of an AXI bus individually.                                               |
| [`axi_slave_compare`](src/axi_slave_compare.sv)      | Compares two slave devices of the same type (and in the same clock domain), returns events on mismatch. |


### Simulation-Only Modules

In addition to the modules above, which are available in synthesis and simulation, the following modules are available only in simulation.  Those modules are widely used in our testbenches, but they are also suitable to build testbenches for AXI modules and systems outside this repository.

| Name                                                 | Description                                                                                            |
|------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [`axi_chan_compare`](src/axi_chan_compare.sv)        | Non-synthesizable module comparing two AXI channels of the same type                                   |
| [`axi_chan_logger`](src/axi_test.sv)                 | Logs the transactions of an AXI4(+ATOPs) port to files.                                                |
| [`axi_driver`](src/axi_test.sv)                      | Low-level driver for AXI4(+ATOPs) that can send and receive individual beats on any channel.           |
| [`axi_dumper`](src/axi_dumper.sv)                    | Dumps log to file to be interpreted by `axi_dumper_interpret` script for debugging purposes.           |
| [`axi_file_master`](src/axi_test.sv)                 | AXI4 master for file-based testbenches                                                                 |
| [`axi_lite_driver`](src/axi_test.sv)                 | Low-level driver for AXI4-Lite that can send and receive individual beats on any channel.              |
| [`axi_lite_rand_master`](src/axi_test.sv)            | AXI4-Lite master component that issues random transactions within user-defined constraints.            |
| [`axi_lite_rand_slave`](src/axi_test.sv)             | AXI4-Lite slave component that responds to transactions with constrainable random delays and data.     |
| [`axi_rand_master`](src/axi_test.sv)                 | AXI4(+ATOPs) master component that issues random transactions within user-defined constraints.         |
| [`axi_rand_slave`](src/axi_test.sv)                  | AXI4(+ATOPs) slave component that responds to transactions with constrainable random delays and data.  |
| [`axi_scoreboard`](src/axi_test.sv)                  | Scoreboard that models a memory that only gets changed by the monitored AXI4(+ATOPs) port.             |
| [`axi_sim_mem`](src/axi_sim_mem.sv)                  | Infinite memory with AXI4 slave port.                                                                  |



## Atomic Operations

AXI4+ATOPs means the full AXI4 specification plus atomic operations (ATOPs) as defined in Section E1.1 of the [AMBA 5 specification][AMBA 5 Spec]. This has the following implications for modules that do not implement ATOPs and systems that include such modules:

- Masters that do not issue ATOPs must set `aw_atop` to `'0`.
- Slaves that do not support ATOPs must specify this in their interface documentation and can ignore the `aw_atop` signal.
- System designers are responsible for ensuring that
  1. slaves that do not support ATOPs are behind an [`axi_atop_filter`](src/axi_atop_filter.sv) if any master could issue an ATOP to such slaves and
  2. the `aw_atop` signal is well-defined at the input of any (non-AXI4-Lite) module in this repository.

Masters and slaves that do support ATOPs must adhere to Section E1.1 of the [AMBA 5 specification][AMBA 5 Spec].  In particular:
- ATOPs that have the `aw_atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP]` bit set generate a write response (B channel) beat and at least one read response (R channel) beat.  All modules for which the `aw_atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP]` bit could be set at their master port must be able to handle both B and R beats (in any order and without requiring a simultaneous handshake) for each such ATOP request.  All modules for which the `aw_atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP]` bit could be set at their slave port must respond with the appropriate number of B and R beats for each such ATOP request.
- ATOPs must not use the same AXI ID as any other transaction that is outstanding at the same time.


## Which EDA Tools Are Supported?

Our code is written in standard SystemVerilog ([IEEE 1800-2012][], to be precise), so the more important question is: Which subset of SystemVerilog does your EDA tool support?

We aim to be compatible with a wide range of EDA tools.  For this reason, we strive to use as simple language constructs as possible, especially for our synthesizable modules.  We encourage contributions that further simplify our code to make it compatible with even more EDA tools.  We also welcome contributions that work around problems that specific EDA tools may have with our code, as long as:
- the EDA tool is reasonably widely used,
- recent versions of the EDA tool are affected,
- the workaround does not break functionality in other tools, and
- the workaround does not significantly complicate code or add maintenance overhead.

In addition, we suggest to report issues with the SystemVerilog language support directly to the EDA vendor. Our code is fully open and
can / should be shared with the EDA vendor as a testcase for any language problem encountered.

All code in each release and on the default branch is tested on a recent version of at least one industry-standard RTL simulator and synthesizer.  You can examine the [CI settings](./.gitlab-ci.yml) to find out which version of which tool we are running.


[AMBA 5 Spec]: https://developer.arm.com/documentation/ihi0022/hc
[IEEE 1800-2012]: https://standards.ieee.org/standard/1800-2012.html
[doc.axi_id_remap]: https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master/module.axi_id_remap
[doc.axi_id_serialize]: https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master/module.axi_id_serialize
[doc.axi_iw_converter]: https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master/module.axi_iw_converter
[doc.axi_lite_regs]: https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master/module.axi_lite_regs

4	// compliance with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
5	// http://solderpad.org/licenses/SHL-0.51. Unless required by applicable law
6	// or agreed to in writing, software, hardware and materials distributed under
7	// this License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR
8	// CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
9	// specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
10	//
11	// Authors:
12	// - Wolfgang Roenninger <wroennin@iis.ee.ethz.ch>
13	// - Andreas Kurth <akurth@iis.ee.ethz.ch>
14	// - Florian Zaruba <zarubaf@iis.ee.ethz.ch>
15	
16	/// axi_xbar: Fully-connected AXI4+ATOP crossbar with an arbitrary number of slave and master ports.
17	/// See `doc/axi_xbar.md` for the documentation, including the definition of parameters and ports.
18	module axi_xbar
19	import cf_math_pkg::idx_width;
20	#(
21	  /// Configuration struct for the crossbar see `axi_pkg` for fields and definitions.
22	  parameter axi_pkg::xbar_cfg_t Cfg                                   = '0,
23	  /// Enable atomic operations support.
24	  parameter bit  ATOPs                                                = 1'b1,
25	  /// Connectivity matrix
26	  parameter bit [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0][Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0] Connectivity = '1,
27	  /// AXI4+ATOP AW channel struct type for the slave ports.
28	  parameter type slv_aw_chan_t                                        = logic,
29	  /// AXI4+ATOP AW channel struct type for the master ports.
30	  parameter type mst_aw_chan_t                                        = logic,
31	  /// AXI4+ATOP W channel struct type for all ports.
32	  parameter type w_chan_t                                             = logic,
33	  /// AXI4+ATOP B channel struct type for the slave ports.
34	  parameter type slv_b_chan_t                                         = logic,
35	  /// AXI4+ATOP B channel struct type for the master ports.
36	  parameter type mst_b_chan_t                                         = logic,
37	  /// AXI4+ATOP AR channel struct type for the slave ports.
38	  parameter type slv_ar_chan_t                                        = logic,
39	  /// AXI4+ATOP AR channel struct type for the master ports.
40	  parameter type mst_ar_chan_t                                        = logic,
41	  /// AXI4+ATOP R channel struct type for the slave ports.
42	  parameter type slv_r_chan_t                                         = logic,
43	  /// AXI4+ATOP R channel struct type for the master ports.
44	  parameter type mst_r_chan_t                                         = logic,
45	  /// AXI4+ATOP request struct type for the slave ports.
46	  parameter type slv_req_t                                            = logic,
47	  /// AXI4+ATOP response struct type for the slave ports.
48	  parameter type slv_resp_t                                           = logic,
49	  /// AXI4+ATOP request struct type for the master ports.
50	  parameter type mst_req_t                                            = logic,
51	  /// AXI4+ATOP response struct type for the master ports
52	  parameter type mst_resp_t                                           = logic,
53	  /// Address rule type for the address decoders from `common_cells:addr_decode`.
54	  /// Example types are provided in `axi_pkg`.
55	  /// Required struct fields:
56	  /// ```
57	  /// typedef struct packed {
58	  ///   int unsigned idx;
59	  ///   axi_addr_t   start_addr;
60	  ///   axi_addr_t   end_addr;
61	  /// } rule_t;
62	  /// ```
63	  parameter type rule_t                                               = axi_pkg::xbar_rule_64_t,
64	  localparam int unsigned MstPortsIdxWidth =
65	      (Cfg.NoMstPorts == 32'd1) ? 32'd1 : unsigned'($clog2(Cfg.NoMstPorts))
66	) (
67	  /// Clock, positive edge triggered.
68	  input  logic                                                          clk_i,
69	  /// Asynchronous reset, active low.
70	  input  logic                                                          rst_ni,
71	  /// Testmode enable, active high.
72	  input  logic                                                          test_i,
73	  /// AXI4+ATOP requests to the slave ports.
74	  input  slv_req_t  [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0]                                slv_ports_req_i,
75	  /// AXI4+ATOP responses of the slave ports.
76	  output slv_resp_t [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0]                                slv_ports_resp_o,
77	  /// AXI4+ATOP requests of the master ports.
78	  output mst_req_t  [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0]                                mst_ports_req_o,
79	  /// AXI4+ATOP responses to the master ports.
80	  input  mst_resp_t [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0]                                mst_ports_resp_i,
81	  /// Address map array input for the crossbar. This map is global for the whole module.
82	  /// It is used for routing the transactions to the respective master ports.
83	  /// Each master port can have multiple different rules.
84	  input  rule_t     [Cfg.NoAddrRules-1:0]                               addr_map_i,
85	  /// Enable default master port.
86	  input  logic      [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0]                                en_default_mst_port_i,
87	  /// Enables a default master port for each slave port. When this is enabled unmapped
88	  /// transactions get issued at the master port given by `default_mst_port_i`.
89	  /// When not used, tie to `'0`.
90	  input  logic      [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0][MstPortsIdxWidth-1:0]          default_mst_port_i
91	);
92	
93	  // signals into the axi_muxes, are of type slave as the multiplexer extends the ID
94	  slv_req_t  [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0][Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0] mst_reqs;
95	  slv_resp_t [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0][Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0] mst_resps;
96	
97	  axi_xbar_unmuxed #(
98	    .Cfg          (Cfg),
99	    .ATOPs        (ATOPs),
100	    .Connectivity (Connectivity),
101	    .aw_chan_t    (slv_aw_chan_t),
102	    .w_chan_t     (w_chan_t),
103	    .b_chan_t     (slv_b_chan_t),
104	    .ar_chan_t    (slv_ar_chan_t),
105	    .r_chan_t     (slv_r_chan_t),
106	    .req_t        (slv_req_t),
107	    .resp_t       (slv_resp_t),
108	    .rule_t       (rule_t)
109	  ) i_xbar_unmuxed (
110	    .clk_i,
111	    .rst_ni,
112	    .test_i,
113	    .slv_ports_req_i,
114	    .slv_ports_resp_o,
115	    .mst_ports_req_o  (mst_reqs),
116	    .mst_ports_resp_i (mst_resps),
117	    .addr_map_i,
118	    .en_default_mst_port_i,
119	    .default_mst_port_i
120	  );
121	
122	  for (genvar i = 0; i < Cfg.NoMstPorts; i++) begin : gen_mst_port_mux
123	    axi_mux #(
124	      .SlvAxiIDWidth ( Cfg.AxiIdWidthSlvPorts ), // ID width of the slave ports
125	      .slv_aw_chan_t ( slv_aw_chan_t          ), // AW Channel Type, slave ports
126	      .mst_aw_chan_t ( mst_aw_chan_t          ), // AW Channel Type, master port
127	      .w_chan_t      ( w_chan_t               ), //  W Channel Type, all ports
128	      .slv_b_chan_t  ( slv_b_chan_t           ), //  B Channel Type, slave ports
129	      .mst_b_chan_t  ( mst_b_chan_t           ), //  B Channel Type, master port
130	      .slv_ar_chan_t ( slv_ar_chan_t          ), // AR Channel Type, slave ports
131	      .mst_ar_chan_t ( mst_ar_chan_t          ), // AR Channel Type, master port
132	      .slv_r_chan_t  ( slv_r_chan_t           ), //  R Channel Type, slave ports
133	      .mst_r_chan_t  ( mst_r_chan_t           ), //  R Channel Type, master port
134	      .slv_req_t     ( slv_req_t              ),
135	      .slv_resp_t    ( slv_resp_t             ),
136	      .mst_req_t     ( mst_req_t              ),
137	      .mst_resp_t    ( mst_resp_t             ),
138	      .NoSlvPorts    ( Cfg.NoSlvPorts         ), // Number of Masters for the module
139	      .MaxWTrans     ( Cfg.MaxSlvTrans        ),
140	      .FallThrough   ( Cfg.FallThrough        ),
141	      .SpillAw       ( Cfg.LatencyMode[4]     ),
142	      .SpillW        ( Cfg.LatencyMode[3]     ),
143	      .SpillB        ( Cfg.LatencyMode[2]     ),
144	      .SpillAr       ( Cfg.LatencyMode[1]     ),
145	      .SpillR        ( Cfg.LatencyMode[0]     )
146	    ) i_axi_mux (
147	      .clk_i,   // Clock
148	      .rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
149	      .test_i,  // Test Mode enable
150	      .slv_reqs_i  ( mst_reqs[i]         ),
151	      .slv_resps_o ( mst_resps[i]        ),
152	      .mst_req_o   ( mst_ports_req_o[i]  ),
153	      .mst_resp_i  ( mst_ports_resp_i[i] )
154	    );
155	  end
156	
157	endmodule
158	
159	`include "axi/assign.svh"
160	`include "axi/typedef.svh"
161	
162	module axi_xbar_intf
163	import cf_math_pkg::idx_width;
164	#(
165	  parameter int unsigned AXI_USER_WIDTH =  0,
166	  parameter axi_pkg::xbar_cfg_t Cfg     = '0,
167	  parameter bit ATOPS                   = 1'b1,
168	  parameter bit [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0][Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0] CONNECTIVITY = '1,
169	  parameter type rule_t                 = axi_pkg::xbar_rule_64_t,
170	  localparam int unsigned MstPortsIdxWidth =
171	        (Cfg.NoMstPorts == 32'd1) ? 32'd1 : unsigned'($clog2(Cfg.NoMstPorts))
172	) (
173	  input  logic                                                      clk_i,
174	  input  logic                                                      rst_ni,
175	  input  logic                                                      test_i,
176	  AXI_BUS.Slave                                                     slv_ports [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0],
177	  AXI_BUS.Master                                                    mst_ports [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0],
178	  input  rule_t [Cfg.NoAddrRules-1:0]                               addr_map_i,
179	  input  logic  [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0]                                en_default_mst_port_i,
180	  input  logic  [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0][MstPortsIdxWidth-1:0]          default_mst_port_i
181	);
182	
183	  localparam int unsigned AxiIdWidthMstPorts = Cfg.AxiIdWidthSlvPorts + $clog2(Cfg.NoSlvPorts);
184	
185	  typedef logic [AxiIdWidthMstPorts     -1:0] id_mst_t;
186	  typedef logic [Cfg.AxiIdWidthSlvPorts -1:0] id_slv_t;
187	  typedef logic [Cfg.AxiAddrWidth       -1:0] addr_t;
188	  typedef logic [Cfg.AxiDataWidth       -1:0] data_t;
189	  typedef logic [Cfg.AxiDataWidth/8     -1:0] strb_t;
190	  typedef logic [AXI_USER_WIDTH         -1:0] user_t;
191	
192	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AW_CHAN_T(mst_aw_chan_t, addr_t, id_mst_t, user_t)
193	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AW_CHAN_T(slv_aw_chan_t, addr_t, id_slv_t, user_t)
194	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_W_CHAN_T(w_chan_t, data_t, strb_t, user_t)
195	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_B_CHAN_T(mst_b_chan_t, id_mst_t, user_t)
196	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_B_CHAN_T(slv_b_chan_t, id_slv_t, user_t)
197	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AR_CHAN_T(mst_ar_chan_t, addr_t, id_mst_t, user_t)
198	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AR_CHAN_T(slv_ar_chan_t, addr_t, id_slv_t, user_t)
199	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(mst_r_chan_t, data_t, id_mst_t, user_t)
200	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(slv_r_chan_t, data_t, id_slv_t, user_t)
201	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(mst_req_t, mst_aw_chan_t, w_chan_t, mst_ar_chan_t)
202	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(slv_req_t, slv_aw_chan_t, w_chan_t, slv_ar_chan_t)
203	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(mst_resp_t, mst_b_chan_t, mst_r_chan_t)
204	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(slv_resp_t, slv_b_chan_t, slv_r_chan_t)
205	
206	  mst_req_t   [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0]  mst_reqs;
207	  mst_resp_t  [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0]  mst_resps;
208	  slv_req_t   [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0]  slv_reqs;
209	  slv_resp_t  [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0]  slv_resps;
210	
211	  for (genvar i = 0; i < Cfg.NoMstPorts; i++) begin : gen_assign_mst
212	    `AXI_ASSIGN_FROM_REQ(mst_ports[i], mst_reqs[i])
213	    `AXI_ASSIGN_TO_RESP(mst_resps[i], mst_ports[i])
214	  end
215

217	    `AXI_ASSIGN_TO_REQ(slv_reqs[i], slv_ports[i])
218	    `AXI_ASSIGN_FROM_RESP(slv_ports[i], slv_resps[i])
219	  end

221	  axi_xbar #(
